question.text: Under REIT Regulations, within how many days from the end of the financial year must the annual meeting of unit holders be held, and what is the maximum gap between two meetings?

answer.text: Within 180 days from FY end; gap shall not exceed 18 months
Within 120 days from FY end; gap shall not exceed 15 months
Within 60 days from FY end; gap shall not exceed 15 months
Within 90 days from FY end; gap shall not exceed 12 months

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under REIT Regulations, an annual meeting of all unit holders shall be held not less than once a year within one hundred twenty days from the end of financial year and the time between two meetings shall not exceed fifteen months. The meeting can be held through audio-video visual means. **The Trap:** 120 days = approximately 4 months. The 15-month gap ensures meetings happen at least annually with some flexibility. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Both REITs and InvITs: annual meeting within 120 days of FY end, maximum 15-month gap between meetings. Can be conducted via audio-video means.
